manager.positiveIntent: Keep the day from going off the rails so I can be reliable for the people counting on me — Sam, Mei, the team.
manager.fearedOutcome: Letting something slip and watching Mei notice — the small flicker of "oh, you didn't" that I can't un-see.
manager.whatItProtects: A quieter part of me that goes blank when too many things are happening at once.
manager.userNotes: Loudest on Sunday evenings reviewing the week ahead, and on Monday mornings when the inbox loads. Quieter on Friday afternoons, almost absent on long walks.

protector.positiveIntent: Make sure no one I care about — Sam, Riley, Mom on the phone — feels let down or under-prioritized.
protector.fearedOutcome: Saying the wrong thing, or saying nothing, and losing the warmth of a friendship I depend on. Riley's text sitting unread for the third week.
protector.whatItProtects: A part of me that just wants to be liked, that has a hard time when someone is even mildly disappointed.
protector.userNotes: Says yes before the rest of me has caught up, especially to weekend invites. The resentment that arrives a day or two later is the tell.

firefighter.positiveIntent: Stop the buildup. Make the heaviness pause for an hour even if nothing actually gets done.
firefighter.fearedOutcome: Sitting with a feeling that doesn't have anywhere to go — being trapped inside it without an exit.
firefighter.whatItProtects: An exhausted part that doesn't believe rest is permitted unless everything is finished, which it never is.
firefighter.userNotes: Shows up around 9pm with my phone in my hand. The scrolling isn't fun by 10:30 but it's easier than going to bed and admitting the day is over.

exile.positiveIntent: Be safe. Not be the one called on without the answer.
exile.fearedOutcome: Being seen having forgotten — the specific sinking of being graded by someone whose opinion matters.
exile.whatItProtects: (itself — the youngest of the parts, the one the others are organized around keeping out of the room)
exile.userNotes: Surfaces when someone asks something completely benign about whether I did a thing. The grown-up voice answers in a second; this part has already been there for a few seconds.
